task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Liverpool Cancer iChart application is launched and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to Mercaptopurine in the Cancer Drugs list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app's 'Cancer Drugs' section is open, and 'Mercaptopurine' is selected or actively being viewed.

task_completion.description: Check the interaction traffic light color for Allopurinol
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app displays an interaction result specifically for Mercaptopurine and Allopurinol, showing a traffic light color indicator (expected to be Red or Orange).

task_completion.description: Check the interaction traffic light color for Colchicine
task_completion.visual_evidence: The app displays an interaction result specifically for Mercaptopurine and Colchicine, showing a traffic light color indicator (expected to be Green or Yellow).
A text editor or terminal is visible showing the creation or editing of a file named 'gout_safety_check.txt' in the '/sdcard/' directory.

task_completion.description: Record the interaction colors for both drugs in the text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the exact lines 'Mercaptopurine + Allopurinol: [COLOR]' and 'Mercaptopurine + Colchicine: [COLOR]', where the colors match what was shown in the app.

task_completion.description: Record the recommendation for the safer drug in the text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains the line 'Recommendation: Use Colchicine' (or the drug corresponding to the safer traffic light color).

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Liverpool Cancer iChart app GUI to find the interactions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine navigation through the app's graphical interface, rather than using a web browser to search for the answer or terminal commands to query an external API.

integrity.description: The agent viewed the actual interaction screens before answering
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be screenshots showing the specific interaction result screens for both drug combinations within the app, proving the agent didn't just guess the colors from prior medical knowledge.

integrity.description: The text file was populated after checking the app
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file creation and writing process should appear in screenshots chronologically after the app interactions have been completed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Mercaptopurine and Allopurinol produce a severe, high-risk interaction (typically Red or Orange traffic light in the app). Colchicine does not inhibit the relevant pathway and is a safer alternative (typically Green or Yellow traffic light). The correct recommendation is Colchicine.
